title: Spring Framework
description: Essential Spring Framework commands and configurations for developers.

commands.description: Marks a configuration class that declares one or more @Bean methods and triggers auto-configuration and component scanning.
commands.scenario: Use when setting up a Spring Boot application to enable auto-configuration.

commands.description: Marks a constructor, field, setter method, or config method as to be autowired by Spring's dependency injection facilities.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to inject a bean into another bean in your Spring application.

commands.description: Combines @Controller and @ResponseBody, indicating that the data returned by each method will be written straight into the response body.
commands.scenario: Use when creating RESTful web services in a Spring application.

commands.description: Handles HTTP GET requests matched with the specified URI path.
commands.scenario: Use to map GET requests to specific handler methods in a Spring controller.

commands.description: Specifies that the class is an entity and is mapped to a database table.
commands.scenario: Use when defining a JPA entity in a Spring application.

commands.description: Demarcates transaction boundaries at the method level.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to ensure that a method executes within a transactional context.

commands.description: Configures the database URL for a Spring Boot application.
commands.scenario: Use when setting up a database connection in a Spring Boot application.

commands.description: Runs a Spring Boot application using Maven.
commands.scenario: Use to start your Spring Boot application during development.

commands.description: Indicates that an annotated class is a "component" and is eligible for component scanning.
commands.scenario: Use to declare a Spring-managed component without specifying a stereotype.

commands.description: Maps HTTP requests to handler methods of MVC and REST controllers.
commands.scenario: Use to map web requests to specific handler methods in a Spring controller.

commands.description: Indicates that a class declares one or more @Bean methods and may be processed by the Spring container to generate bean definitions.
commands.scenario: Use when defining configuration classes in a Spring application.

commands.description: Indicates that a method produces a bean to be managed by the Spring container.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to define a bean in a Spring configuration class.

commands.description: Starter for building web, including RESTful, applications using Spring MVC. Uses Tomcat as the default embedded container.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to add web capabilities to your Spring Boot application.

commands.description: Indicates that an annotated class is a "Service", originally defined by Domain-Driven Design (Evans, 2003) as "an operation offered as an interface that stands alone in the model, with no encapsulated state."
commands.scenario: Use to declare a service layer component in a Spring application.

commands.description: Indicates that an annotated class is a "Repository", which is an abstraction of data access and storage.
commands.scenario: Use to declare a data access layer component in a Spring application.
